narratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically American) foreign policy and perception, portraying it as hysterical, exaggerated, and fundamentally dishonest in its portrayal of Russia as a threat.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narratives.reasoning: The text predicts and seemingly advocates for a catastrophic nuclear war as a consequence of Western political actions, framing it as an inevitable negative outcome brought about by Western 'un-elected bureaucrats.'

narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out
narratives.reasoning: This statement explicitly evokes fear and anxiety about the ultimate consequence of the conflict: total nuclear annihilation. It presents this as a plausible or even desired outcome to end the current situation.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly questions the reliability and motives of Western media, accusing it of deceptive framing ('as if Russia is losing') that has harmful real-world consequences, thereby undermining trust in these media sources.

narratives.evidence_quote: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
narratives.reasoning: The title and premise of the discussion are built on speculating about the outcome of the war ('Russia is Winning') and the potential consequences ('Nuclear Threat').

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses the West (specifically the US) of consistently and disproportionately exaggerating threats from Russia and China, fitting the definition of portraying the West's reaction as disproportionate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This implies the West, through its media, is deliberately prolonging the war for its own reasons (e.g., to weaken Russia), disregarding the resulting Ukrainian deaths, which aligns with the subnarrative that the West is using Ukraine for its own benefit.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: The statement suggests that the current Western approach (media framing) is actively preventing successful negotiations, thereby discrediting the potential of diplomatic efforts to end the war.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: I do not think an amerikan life is worth 1/2 as much as an African (or any other life outside the west) ..they are trash ..not worth the effort ..get to a point of “kill them all” either because they do not care and they want us all dead ..or because they just sit back and “DO NOT CARE”
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative of discrediting the West by portraying Western (specifically American) populations as morally bankrupt, uncaring, and deserving of destruction. This extreme dehumanization and condemnation of Western citizens is a distinct argument that does not fit any of the more specific diplomatic subnarratives provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing …
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote argues that Western (specifically American) demonization and overreaction to Russia is a persistent, misguided policy. It implies that this aggressive posture, which includes sanctions and confrontation, is based on a false premise ('SELDOM a threat'), suggesting the Western strategy is fundamentally flawed and will lead to negative, self-inflicted consequences.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement directly attributes negative consequences (the implied path to nuclear war and societal collapse) to the actions of Western governments and their unelected officials. It supports the parent narrative by blaming Western political structures for creating a catastrophic situation, which is a consequence for the West itself, but does not fit the specific definitions of sanctions backfiring or refugee flows.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons, framing a full nuclear war as a seemingly imminent or desired outcome to end the conflict, which aligns with the subnarrative definition.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion..
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the parent narrative of amplifying war-related fears by arguing that Western fear of Russia is historically exaggerated and manufactured, which contributes to a climate of fear and tension, but does not fit any of the other specific subnarrative definitions provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' explicitly framing it as a tool for disseminating biased or false information, which matches the definition of discrediting Western media as an instrument of propaganda.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the parent narrative of distrust towards media by accusing Western media of a specific, harmful misrepresentation (framing Russia as losing) that has dire consequences (prolonging war, causing death). It discredits Western media's reporting but does not fit the more specific definitions of being an 'instrument of propaganda' or targeting Ukrainian media, thus it is a remainder supporting the parent narrative.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Ukrainian army is collapsing
subnarratives.reasoning: The title and opening statement directly claim that Russia is winning the war, which inherently implies the opposing Ukrainian forces are losing or collapsing, fitting the definition of suggesting a decline in Ukrainian military effectiveness.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement counters Western media narratives about Russia losing, thereby engaging in speculation about war outcomes (i.e., that Russia is not losing). It supports the parent narrative of speculating on war outcomes but does not fit the specific definitions about army collapse or loss of territory.
